title: Public deterministic playable Worker advance helper
summary: Expose a legal public root Worker/session helper so Web can advance the authoritative WM-0150 playable scenario without importing sim-core or hardcoding the internal Noop command-id convention.
acceptance: A public root @wuming-town/sim-worker helper or BrowserSimulationWorkerSession method lets Web advance the WM-0150 playable scenario to an explicit logical target tick without importing @wuming-town/sim-core
The helper uses the existing Worker PlayerCommandBatch path and does not add a new top-level Worker message family unless ADR-0015 is superseded with a stricter reason

apps/web consumes only @wuming-town/sim-worker and @wuming-town/sim-protocol public roots through apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.ts; no app code imports sim-core, worker internals, or hardcodes/parses wm0150.advance command ids
SetSpeed/Pause behavior is documented as not being autonomous browser ticking for this precursor, or any larger clock-loop change is covered by a superseding ADR and tests

Worker tests prove init -> public advance -> UiDeltaPayload.playable tick progression, and accepted PrioritizeLampWork / QueueSimpleBuild commands followed by public advance requests progress job, pawn, build/lamp and resource projection state
Reliable CommandResult, SaveReady and fatal/error delivery remains separate from projection delivery after larger or repeated advance-driven UiDelta payloads

A real Chromium worker-smoke proves the public root browser session/helper path advances WM-0150 from tick 0 and produces updated PlayableProjectionV1 without direct sim-core imports in the smoke module
No product HUD, placement UI, localization rewrite, renderer-pixi feature implementation, public release, telemetry/account surface, or public save compatibility promise is added

Save/load stance is explicit: advance requests are active-session deterministic inputs only; in-progress playable command persistence remains unsupported unless a later versioned save task adds focused sections and migration/fixture evidence
The report states the exact public helper/session API name and the exact WM-0152 resume path

history.details: Created to unblock WM-0152 after confirming WM-0157/WM-0158 provide command/projection paths but no legal public way for Web to advance the authoritative playable Worker tick.

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. Advance requests are deterministic active-session inputs only; in-progress playable command persistence remains unsupported until a separate versioned save/load task is approved.
forbiddenPaths: apps/web importing @wuming-town/sim-core, @wuming-town/sim-worker subpaths, or packages/*/src internals
apps/web hardcoding or parsing wm0150.advance command-id strings
product HUD, placement UI, renderer-pixi, ui-react, localization, shell-bootstrap feature implementation beyond a thin simulation-worker-session adapter
UI-owned authoritative ticking, job state, pawn motion, resource counts, build progress, action availability or blocked reasons
hidden global Worker/session singleton or timer singleton
new autonomous browser clock loop via SetSpeed/Pause unless ADR-0015 is superseded with lifecycle/backpressure/replay semantics
new top-level Worker message family unless ADR-0015 is superseded with a stricter reason
new public save compatibility, migration or release promises
new runtime dependencies without a separate ADR
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback surfaces
